Document 1: Every Veteran Counts Act - Directs the Secretary of Commerce to include on each questionnaire used in a decennial census a question regarding whether an individual is a veteran.
authorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to report to Congress identifying the laws or programs administered by the VA.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to deem any person who has received an award from the U.S. Armed Forces for engagement in active combat to have satisfied specified naturalization requirements.
Document 4: Department of Veterans Affairs Management Accountability Act of 2014 - Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to remove any individual from the Senior Executive Service upon determining that such individual's performance warrants such removal.
Document 6: Makes 3,000 immigrant visas in FY2010-FY2012 available to individuals who were born in Tibet and have been continuously residing in India or Nepal.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, accountability, and services of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: Directs the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to consider the economic effects on the community served by such post office.
Establishes a waiting period and appeal procedures for USPS determinations to close or consolidate mail processing facilities.
Document 2: Requires the U.S. Postal Service to bargain with unions representing career Postal Service employees before entering into a contract for mail processing.
Cost over $5 million.
Document 3: Prohibits the United States Postal Service (USPS) from closing any post office that does not have another post office within eight miles.
Measured by public roads with regular year-round access.
Document 4: Directs the United States Postal Service to issue and sell an Alzheimer's Disease Research Semipostal Stamp.
Proceeds from the sale of the stamp must be transferred to the National Institutes of Health.
Document 5: Requires the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to issue for a four-year period a semipostal to benefit certain Department of Agriculture (USDA) conservation programs.
A semipostal is a postage stamp that is issued and sold by the USPS at a premium.
Document 6: Prohibits the Commissioner from closing or consolidating SSA field or hearing offices.
Requires the Commissioner to submit an annual budget estimate of the Social Security Administration directly to Congress.
Document 7: Requires the Postal Service to ensure that every post office has access to running water and bathroom facilities.
Prohibits the Post Office from considering whether the post office meets the requirements of this Act.

topic.name: LGBTQ+ Rights and Equality Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative efforts aimed at promoting equality and preventing discrimination based on sexual orientation, gender identity, and sex.

Document 1: prohibit the exclusion of citizens from service as a grand or petit juror on account of sexual orientation or gender identity
expanding the current prohibition on exclusion on account of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, or economic status.
Document 2: revises remedies for, enforcement of, and exceptions to prohibitions against sex discrimination in the payment of wages
Prohibits retaliation for inquiring about, discussing, or disclosing the wages of the employee or another employee.
Document 3: Prohibits programs funded under this Act from discriminating on the basis of actual or perceived sex, race, color, ethnicity, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, or religion
emphasis on reducing HIV, other sexually transmitted infections, and unintended pregnancy.
Document 4: invalidates the Dear Colleague Letter on Transgender Students
treatment of transgender students under the prohibitions against sex discrimination
Document 6: includes sex, sexual orientation, and gender identity among the prohibited categories of discrimination
expanding DOJ's existing authority to bring such actions for complaints based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin.
Document 7: identify certain unwelcome conduct of a sexual nature in or around a dwelling
constitute a discriminatory housing practice based on hostile environment sexual harassment.

topic.name: Federal Land Conveyance
topic.description: The documents discuss various acts and measures related to the conveyance of federal land to different entities for specific uses or purposes.

Document 1: All interest of the United States in 148 acres of identified land shall be conveyed by patent to the Gordman-Leverich Partnership.
The conveyance must be completed within 180 days after enactment of this Act.
Document 2: Requires conveyance of approximately 44 acres of federally owned land administered by the Agricultural Research Service.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to the Mullins and Sturgill Cemetery Association specified National Forest System land.
Requires the Association to pay the Secretary cash consideration equal to the land's market value.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior to convey specified federal lands and interests to the Ketchikan Gateway Borough.
Document 5: This bill directs the Department of Health and Human Services to convey to the Tanana Tribal Council all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to certain property.
The conveyance shall not require any consideration from the Council for the property.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey, without consideration, to the state of Utah all interest of the United States in and to certain lands.
Requires that any such determination be made in consultation with the Secretary of Defense and the Governor of Utah.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to Turnabout Ranch all right, title, and interest to such land.
Requires Turnabout Ranch to pay an amount equal to the appraised value of the federal land.

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at the conservation and management of wildlife, particularly migratory birds and aquatic species, through funding, regulations, and habitat protection.

Document 1: Amends the Wetland Loan Act to extend funding for conservation of migratory waterfowl and habitat.
Expresses the sense of Congress that the funds provided pursuant to this Act should be used for preserving and increasing waterfowl populations.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to authorize the stocking of fish in lakes in the North Cascades National Park.
Requires the Secretary to coordinate the stocking of fish with the state of Washington.
Document 3: Amends the Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamp Act to increase the price of duck stamps.
Requires Interior to include in an annual report an assessment of the status of wetlands conservation projects for migratory bird conservation purposes.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue a permit for the importation of polar bear parts taken in a sport hunt in Canada.
Document 5: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to require fishery management plans for overfished fisheries.
Requires consideration of factors other than commercial and recreational fishing in evaluating progress to end overfishing.
Document 6: Prohibits a person from shipping, transporting, or releasing genetically altered finfish into a natural environment.
The Department of Commerce may enforce penalties for violations of this Act under specified provisions of the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act.
Document 7: Amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to provide that nothing in such Act prohibits possessing, selling, or transporting authentic Alaska Native handicraft containing a non-edible migratory bird part.

topic.name: Civil Rights and Legal Protections
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at protecting civil rights, freedom of speech, and legal processes in the United States.

Document 1: remedies for a civil action for deprivation of rights are limited to injunctive and declaratory relief
prohibits the award of attorney's fees
Document 2: Prohibits a domestic court from recognizing or enforcing a foreign judgment for defamation
determines that the defamation law provided at least as much protection for freedom of speech
Document 3: requires the court to impose an appropriate sanction on any attorney, law firm, or party
sanction to compensate parties injured by the conduct in question
Document 5: includes expert witness fees within the definition of 'attorneys' fees'
may be awarded to the prevailing party in a civil action
Document 6: grants immunity from civil liability to persons who report suspicious activity
provides that an authorized official not entitled to assert the defense of qualified immunity shall nonetheless be immune
Document 7: limits the remedy in a civil action against a state or local official for deprivation of civil rights
deny attorneys' fees where the deprivation consists of a violation of a prohibition in the Constitution against the establishment of religion

topic.name: Education and Community Engagement
topic.description: This topic encompasses initiatives and legislation aimed at improving education access, support, and community involvement for youth and families.

Document 1: extend the authorization of appropriations for the Fund for the Improvement of Postsecondary Education
grant funds for costs associated with carrying out such transition programs
Document 2: create a new Ready to Learn program authorizing the Secretary of Education to award grants
include training and materials for educators and caregivers
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to award grants to eligible nonprofit organizations
integrate environmental education, food security, health and fitness, resource management, and job training
Document 4: assist them in providing disconnected youth with the support needed to attain a secondary school diploma
requires each eligible partnership to include representatives from certain state and local service agencies
Document 5: implement mentoring programs that link at-risk youth with responsible adults
gives grant priority to applicants that serve at-risk youth in high crime areas
Document 6: ensure the academic, physical, social, emotional, and civic development of disadvantaged youth
develop and implement a local child and youth strategy
Document 7: require the Secretary of Education to award grants to states for competitive subgrants to local educational agencies
improving family and community involvement in such schools

topic.name: Workforce Development and Education Grants
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts that authorize grants aimed at enhancing workforce skills, education, and training in various professional fields.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to award grants to eligible institutions to establish a master's or doctoral degree program in orthotics and prosthetics.
Requires the Secretary to award a grant to an institution with orthotic and prosthetic research and education experience.
Document 2: Amends the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 to authorize the Secretary of Labor to award competitive grants to local boards, community colleges, and postsecondary vocational institutions.
Promote local economic growth and eliminate gaps between the workforce skills available and the workforce skills needed.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive five-year grants to local educational agencies (LEAs) to recruit, support, and retain highly qualified and effective teachers.
Gives priority to LEAs that have the highest number of disadvantaged students.
Document 4: Directs the Department of Justice's Office of Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention to establish regional training centers affiliated with institutions of higher education.
Develop model undergraduate and graduate child maltreatment curricula for distribution to IHEs.
Document 5: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a Nurse Distance Education Pilot Program through which grants may be awarded.
Promote the study of nursing at all educational levels.
Document 6: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to institutions of higher education to conduct pilot programs.
Requires grantees to make digital course materials available to undergraduate students.
Document 7: Amends the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to carry out a matching grant program.
Requires a qualifying entity to carry out programs that relieve veterinarian shortage situations.

topic.name: Aviation Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing security measures and regulations within the aviation sector in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to prescribe regulations that require the use of security cameras at all screening locations
Prescribes a civil penalty of up to $10,000 for individuals who enter an aircraft or an airport area in violation of security requirements.
Document 2: Amends the FAA Modernization and Reform Act of 2012 to permit an individual to operate a small commercial unmanned aircraft
the aircraft may be operated for commercial purposes only in daylight conditions, under visual line of sight rules.
Document 3: Replaces the Under Secretary of Transportation for Security in the Department of Transportation (DOT) with the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security as head of the Transportation Security Administration (TSA).
Document 4: Transfers from the Under Secretary of Transportation for Security (DOT) to the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) the authority to approve any application of an airport operator to have the screening of passengers and property
Requires the Secretary to approve such an application unless approval would compromise security.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to prescribe regulations to impose a fee on an air carrier that charges a fee for the first bag checked
Requires such fees to be credited as offsetting collections to the Secretary for the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) for aviation security.
Document 6: Reauthorizes for the period October 1, 2015, through March 31, 2016, the airport improvement program.
The FAA Modernization and Reform Act of 2012 is amended to extend through FY2016 the requirement for an Inspector General report on participation in FAA programs.
Document 7: Requires air carriers to refund passenger baggage fees within 30 days after the arrival of any passenger flight in which a piece of baggage is lost, delayed, or damaged.
Makes it an unfair or deceptive practice for a domestic air carrier to sell a ticket for air transportation but fail to inform the ticket purchaser of the cost of checking each piece of baggage.

topic.name: Consumer Product Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing the safety and accessibility of consumer products and public facilities, focusing on compliance with safety standards and regulations.

Document 1: Prohibits any suit brought for an alleged violation of the revised regulations of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 concerning the requirements to provide an accessible means of entry to pools

Directs the AG to revise accessibility regulations to provide that a place of public accommodation or a commercial facility is in compliance with the requirement to have an accessible means of entry to a pool when such a place or facility uses a portable pool lift on request.
Document 2: Amends the Consumer Product Safety Act to make it unlawful to sell a product that is not in conformity with an applicable consumer product safety standard
Requires the manufacturer of a consumer product to place distinguishing marks on the product that will enable the ultimate purchaser to determine the source, date, and cohort of production.
Document 3: Amends the Energy Policy and Conservation Act to exempt a walk-in cooler or walk-in freezer component from the requirement that it contain wall, ceiling, and door insulation if the manufacturer has demonstrated that such component model reduces energy consumption
This document relates to safety standards regarding energy-efficient appliances.
Document 4: Amends the Consumer Product Safety Improvement Act of 2008 to require the Consumer Product Safety Commission to exempt certain all-terrain vehicles from children's product lead limits if compliance is not technologically feasible
Allows the CPSC to exempt a specific product from lead limits if it finds that lead will not result in absorption into the human body.
Document 5: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to issue a revised safety assessment for food containers composed of bisphenol A (BPA)
Deems a food to be misbranded if its container is composed in whole or part of BPA, unless the label includes a specific warning.
Document 6: Amends the Consumer Product Safety Act to authorize appropriations to carry out the Act and for other purposes
Requires third party certification of children's products and provides for tracking and record keeping regarding them.
Document 7: Exempts a lawful seller from liability for personal injury arising out of an accident involving a seller's products unless certain conditions are met
Limits the seller's liability to the personal injury directly caused by activities where a claimant proves negligence.

topic.name: Higher Education Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving access, affordability, and quality of higher education in the United States.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965

Document 2: direct the Secretary of Education to inform students who indicate on their Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) form that they are or were in the foster care system of their potential eligibility for federal student aid
Document 3: directs the Secretary of Education to make competitive grants to institutions of higher education (IHEs) to support pilot programs that expand the use of open textbooks
Document 4: modifies requirements for a proprietary institution of higher education (IHE) to participate in title IV (Student Assistance) federal student aid programs
Document 5: Direct the Secretary of Education to conduct studies regarding the feasibility of, and options for, implementing the Pay It Forward model for funding postsecondary education
Document 6: direct the Secretary of Commerce to establish a Minority Serving Institution Digital and Wireless Technology Opportunity Program
Document 7: directs the Secretary of Education to pay four-year Native American-serving nontribal institutions of higher education the out-of-state tuition of their Indian students

topic.name: Border Security and Infrastructure
topic.description: The common topic focuses on legislation and initiatives aimed at enhancing border security, customs operations, and infrastructure at U.S. ports of entry.

Document 1: Directs the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to hire, train, and assign to duty, by September 30, 2020: 5,000 additional full-time U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) officers.
Authorize CBP to aid in the enforcement of federal customs, immigration, and agriculture laws.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security and the Secretary of State to jointly report to Congress on the implementation of the Western Hemisphere Travel Initiative (WHTI) regarding the U.S.-Canada international land and maritime border.
Specifies that such report include information concerning effects on travel, trade, and border security.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to conduct in the maritime environment a program for the mobile biometric identification of suspected individuals, including terrorists, to enhance border security.
Defines 'biometric identification' as the use of fingerprint and digital photography images.
Document 5: Amends the Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1985 to authorize the Commissioner of the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) to provide additional customs and immigration services at Customs ports of entry.
Requires such persons to pay a fee to reimburse CBP for such services.
Document 7: Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to enter into agreements with persons for the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) to provide customs and immigration services at a land border port of entry.
Prescribes minimum requirements for such procedures.

topic.name: U.S.-Cuba Relations Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating travel, telecommunications, and trade between the United States and Cuba.

Document 1: Prohibits that President from regulating or prohibiting travel to or from Cuba
transactions incident to travel for the purpose of visiting a close relative who is a national of Cuba.
Document 2: Repeals the Radio Broadcasting to Cuba Act
eliminating Voice of America: Radio/Television Marti broadcasts to Cuba.
Document 3: authorizes the President to permit any person subject to U.S. jurisdiction to export consumer communication devices to Cuba

Document 4: declares that oil and gas export restrictions shall not apply to Cuba
permit any person subject to U.S. jurisdiction to export energy resources to Cuba.
Document 7: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to repeal the embargo on trade with Cuba
authorizes the President to impose export controls with respect to Cuba only on unusual threats to U.S. national security.

topic.name: U.S. Military Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, oversight, and funding of the U.S. military and defense initiatives.

Document 5: Restrictions on the use of cluster munitions and cleanup plans.

Document 7: Policy for safe withdrawal of U.S. military personnel from Afghanistan.

topic.name: Energy Regulation and Trade
topic.description: Legislation related to the regulation, export, and consumer protection in the energy sector, particularly oil and natural gas.

Document 1: Amends the Sherman Act to declare it to be illegal for any foreign state to limit the production or distribution of oil.

Document 2: Amends the Energy Policy and Conservation Act to repeal authority to restrict the export of petroleum products.

Document 3: Amends the Natural Gas Act to prohibit exporting natural gas without authorization from the Secretary of Energy.

Document 5: Amends the Federal Trade Commission Act to direct the FTC to determine whether any supplier is charging excessive prices for gasoline.

Document 6: Amends the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act to require states to adopt a net metering standard.
Prohibits states from restricting access to the electric power transmission system.
Document 7: Amends the Commodity Exchange Act to treat utility operations-related swaps differently for regulatory purposes.

topic.name: Legislative Procedures and Authority

Document 4: Requires the President to transmit to the Speaker of the House of Representatives, the chairmen of the House and Senate Committees on the Judiciary, and to the Senate Majority Leader each signing statement that declares or insinuates the President's intention to disregard provisions of any bill he has signed into law.
Document 5: Amends the Revised Statutes with respect to the time of election to establish the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November, in every even numbered year, as the days for the election.
Document 6: Amends the federal criminal code to replace the current two-year ban on lobbying contacts by former Senators and one-year ban on lobbying contacts by former Members of the House of Representatives with an outright ban at any time on such contacts.
Document 7: Prohibits funds from being obligated or otherwise made available for an item (earmark) listed in the joint explanatory statement accompanying the conference report on H.R. 2082.

topic.name: Veterans and Military Health Legislation

Allows such payment in addition to any other authorized Montgomery GI Bill educational assistance.
Document 2: Waives, during FY2009, the basic military pay adjustment tied to increases in the Employment Cost Index

Document 5: provides specialized classroom and on-the-job training in iron working skills to adult members of federally recognized Indian tribes

Document 6: provides that any enterprise or institution owned and operated by an Indian tribe is not considered an employer for purposes of such Act
Document 7: reaffirms the federal government's fiduciary responsibilities to Indians
allows tribes to propose Indian trust asset management plans

topic.name: Employee Leave and Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to employee leave, health care benefits, and paid time off.

Document 1: Health Care Incentive Act
value of creditable health care benefits
Document 2: Department of Veterans Affairs Emergency Medical Staffing Recruitment and Retention Act
modify the hours of employment for a physician or physician assistant
Document 3: Family and Medical Leave Enhancement Act of 2014
amends the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993
Document 4: Family and Medical Leave Inclusion Act
provide for employee leave to care for a domestic partner
Document 5: Guaranteed Paid Vacation Act
require specified employers to provide each eligible employee at least 10 days of paid vacation time
Document 6: Family Fairness Act of 2008
make an employee eligible for coverage under such Act
Document 7: Healthy Families Act
requires certain employers to permit each employee to earn at least one hour of paid sick time

topic.name: Health Insurance Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating health insurance coverage, including provisions for waivers, preexisting conditions, and patient rights.

Document 1: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA) to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish waiver processes
Sets forth PPACA requirements that may be waived, including those related to minimum essential coverage
Document 2: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA) to prohibit a group health plan from imposing any preexisting conditions exclusion
Requires health insurance issuers to charge the same premium price for the same coverage
Document 3: Establishes an Advantage HSA program to provide payments to individuals in lieu of benefits under Medicare, Medicaid, and CHIP
Requires health care providers to disclose to patients the actual price to be charged for services
Document 4: Permits a health insurance issuer that had in effect health insurance coverage in the group market on any date during 2013 to continue offering that coverage
Treats that coverage as a grandfathered health plan
Document 5: Prohibits a group health plan from imposing any preexisting condition exclusion
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to report to Congress concerning the occurrence of adverse selection
Document 6: Prohibits the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) from implementing or enforcing any provision of the final rule related to the coverage of preventive health services
Declares that a health plan shall not be considered to have failed to provide essential health benefits based on religious beliefs
Document 7: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA) to allow a state-authorized public entity benefits pool
Requires the Secretary to approve such a pool if it provides at least the essential health benefits

topic.name: Intelligence Authorization and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative measures and acts related to the authorization, oversight, and management of U.S. intelligence activities and agencies.

Document 1: Amends the National Security Act of 1947 to require the head of an element of the intelligence community to report requests for information to the Attorney General and congressional committees.
Revises the definition of 'covert agent' for purposes of such Act.
Document 2: Establishes a Foreign Intelligence and Information Commission to evaluate and provide recommendations on foreign intelligence collection and reporting.
Document 3: Authorizes FY2017 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of various intelligence agencies.
Specifies that the amounts authorized and personnel ceilings are in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 4: Authorizes FY2014 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence activities of multiple intelligence agencies.
Allows the DNI to authorize employment of civilian personnel in excess of the number authorized when necessary.
Document 5: Amends the National Security Agency Act of 1959 to require the Director of NSA to be appointed by the President with Senate consent.
Document 6: Authorizes appropriations for FY2013 for the conduct of intelligence activities of various intelligence agencies.
Specifies that amounts authorized and personnel ceilings are in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 7: Authorizes FY2017 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence activities, including the management of personnel within the intelligence community.

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing consumer protection, transparency, and accountability within the financial sector.

Document 1: Amends the Paperwork Reduction Act to direct agency heads not to impose civil fines for first-time paperwork violations by small business concerns
Permits an agency to determine that a fine should not be imposed for a violation that presents a danger to public health or safety if the violation is corrected within 24 hours.
Document 2: Requires the Department of Homeland Security to provide an option under the Secure Mail Initiative
For payment of a fee recouping costs, a person may require the U.S. Postal Service to obtain a signature.
Document 3: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act to require consumer reporting agencies to disclose any information in the consumer's file concerning credit scores
Directs the Comptroller General to study bankruptcy risk scores used by consumer reporting agencies.
Document 4: Requires the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to accompany research papers with all studies, data, and analyses on which it was based.
Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010.
Document 5: Prescribes requirements for the consumer complaint website the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) must establish
The CFPB may only make consumer complaint information available to the public in an aggregated format.
Document 6: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act to prohibit a consumer reporting agency from furnishing a consumer report for use in making a decision to underwrite or rate auto insurance
Exempts from this ban property loss data regarding auto insurance.
Document 7: Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to exempt from its annual privacy policy notice requirement any financial institution that has not changed its policies
Provides nonpublic personal information only in accordance with specified requirements.

topic.name: Wilderness Designation and Protection
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on the designation, protection, and management of wilderness areas and natural resources in various states across the United States.

Document 1: Designates peak 12,240 as Mt. Andrea Lawrence.
Located in the Ansel Adams Wilderness and Yosemite National Park.
Document 2: Designates lands in Rocky Mountain National Park as wilderness.
Includes provisions for managing water rights and trail boundaries.
Document 3: Directs a study for the New Philadelphia archaeological site for potential National Park designation.
Considers preservation and interpretation alternatives.
Document 4: Designates National Forest System lands and public lands as wilderness in the Northern Rockies.
Prohibits development that impairs natural qualities.
Document 5: Designates specified lands in Utah as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Includes provisions for managing water rights and land exchanges.
Document 6: Designates federal lands in Cherokee National Forest as wilderness.
Adds to the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Document 7: Establishes the Fort Stanton-Snowy River National Cave Conservation Area.
Requires management consistent with the protection of cave resources.

topic.name: Homeownership Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting homeowners from foreclosure and promoting affordable housing options.

Document 1: establish the right of an eligible deferred-foreclosure consumer to defer initiation of a foreclosure
prohibits a creditor or servicer from imposing any late fee or other fee or charge during the deferment period.
Document 2: require the Federal National Mortgage Association and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation to disclose promptly to the prospective seller the amount by which the purchase price was less than the minimum purchase price
directs the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to require transparency in short sale transactions.
Document 3: provides a 50% discount for teachers and public safety officers purchasing certain eligible asset properties
makes a public safety officer or teacher eligible to purchase any property owned or held by the Secretary.
Document 4: authorize a mortgagor of a property subject to a federally related mortgage loan to file a motion before a court to stay any foreclosure proceedings
prohibit the assessment or collection of any late fees regarding payments on such loan.
Document 5: requires the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to establish a pilot program to encourage the use of shared equity mortgage modifications
designed to return greater net present value to investors than other loss-mitigation activities, including foreclosure.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving Medicare and Medicaid services, including transportation, prescription drug plans, healthcare access, and end-of-life care management.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to publish an interim final rule to revise certain Medicaid transportation regulations

to allow a state plan under title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to provide, at state option, reimbursement for costs attributable to providing no-load volunteer travel services to Medicaid-eligible individuals
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide for enhanced penalties for Medicare Advantage-Prescription Drug (MA-PD) plans
public notification about plans that habitually receive intermediate sanctions
Document 3: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to require drug manufacturers to pay rebates to state prescription drug discount programs
as a condition of participating in a Medicaid rebate agreement for outpatient prescription drugs
Document 4: Amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to provide for Medicare and Medicaid coverage of advanced illness care management services
to conduct a national public education campaign on planning for care near the end of life
Document 5: It is the intent of Congress to continue to study and request input on the effects of socioeconomic status and dual-eligible populations on the five-star quality rating system for Medicare Advantage (MA) plans
delay Centers for Medicare & Medicaid (CMS) authority to terminate MA plan contracts solely on the basis of performance under the five-star rating system
Document 6: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
to require incurred costs to include the negotiated price of a covered part D drug
Document 7: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide for Medicare coverage of certain medical mobility devices approved as a class III medical device
pursuant to a premarket approval application under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at protecting consumers from unfair or deceptive practices in commerce.

Document 1: amends the Federal Trade Commission Act to require the FTC to submit an annual report about its investigations concerning unfair or deceptive acts
prohibits the FTC from identifying a person who is the subject of an investigation
Document 2: Makes it unlawful to knowingly manufacture, market, sell, ship, or otherwise provide to another individual any product with the intent to assist such individual to use such product to defraud a drug test
Treats a violation of this Act as an unfair or deceptive act or practice under the Federal Trade Commission Act
Document 3: directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate rules for the primary or secondary sale, distribution, or pricing of tickets
prohibit the use of software to circumvent a security system or access control measure on a primary ticket seller's website
Document 4: Prohibits any state from imposing a discriminatory tax on any means of providing multichannel video programming distribution services
Defines a tax as discriminatory if the net tax imposed on one means of providing multichannel video service is higher than the net tax rate imposed on another
Document 5: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate rules to prohibit, as an unfair and deceptive act or practice, a person from advertising with the intent to deceptively create the impression that they are a provider of abortion services
Defines 'abortion services' as providing surgical and non-surgical procedures to terminate a pregnancy
Document 6: Requires business entities that initiate or receive certain customer service communications to disclose their physical location if located outside the United States
Treats such a business entity's failure to comply as a regulatory violation under the Federal Trade Commission Act regarding unfair or deceptive acts or practices
Document 7: prohibits states or local governments from imposing any new discriminatory tax on or with respect to mobile services
requires the Government Accountability Office to study and report on the impact of state and local taxes on mobile services, providers, or property on the costs consumers pay for mobile services

topic.name: Retirement and Health Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation focused on the regulation and improvement of retirement plans and health benefits for employees and their beneficiaries.

Document 1: exempt such benefits paid to eligible beneficiaries from applicable employment and unemployment taxes
allow self-employed individuals a tax deduction for the health insurance costs of their eligible beneficiaries
Document 2: require assets of single-employer pension plans to be held in trust by joint boards of trustees
sets forth additional requirements for plan termination
Document 3: suspends for calendar 2008-2010 the minimum required distributions from certain individual retirement (IRA) plans
Document 4: deny tax benefits relating to bonds issued by a state or political subdivision during any period in which such state or political subdivision is noncompliant with specified reporting requirements
requires plan sponsors of a state or local government employee pension benefit plan to file with the Secretary of the Treasury a report
Document 5: extend through 2010 the suspension of minimum distribution rules for tax-deferred retirement plans
Document 6: require the fiduciary of an employee benefit plan to establish policies for appointment of an investment manager
consider the concentration level of the plan's exposure to firm-specific risks
Document 7: require a group health plan that provides medical and surgical benefits as well as benefits for prosthetic devices and components to offer such prosthetic and orthotic coverage
prohibits separate financial requirements or more restrictive treatment limitations

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating financial institutions and enhancing consumer protections within the financial sector.

Document 1: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 relating to prohibitions on proprietary trading
prohibits any construction of these prohibitions to require the divestiture of collateralized debt obligations
Document 2: Amends the Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council Act of 1978
to require federal financial institution regulatory agencies to establish a single, toll-free telephone number for consumer complaints
Document 3: Revises the Community Reinvestment Act of 1977 with regard to assessments of regulated financial institutions
specifies the community development and other related activities of a regulated financial institution
Document 4: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to delay the effective date for prohibitions against proprietary trading
delays the effective date of such prohibitions until the Secretary of the Treasury identifies foreign countries
Document 5: Amends the Federal Home Loan Bank Act to modify the eligibility criteria for membership
redefining insured depository institution to cover certain insured depository institutions
Document 6: Establishes the Federal Accounting Oversight Board to approve and oversee accounting principles
directs the FAOB to report annually to Congress on the principles and standards it has reviewed
Document 7: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to authorize the Financial Stability Oversight Council
to subject a bank holding company to enhanced supervision and prudential standards

topic.name: Child Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving child care services and support for families with young children.

Document 1: Diaper Investment and Aid to Promote Economic Recovery Act or DIAPER Act - Amends the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 to include a state's provision under a child care and development block grant of diapers and diapering supplies to enrolled child care providers as covered child care assistance to parents with children under age three.
Document 2: Anthony DeJuan Boatwright Act - Amends the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 to require the state plans for the use of child care and development block grant funds to include... a recommendation to each such provider that it carry liability insurance covering the operation of its child care business.
Document 3: Starting Early Starting Right Act - Amends the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 to establish, within the Administration for Children and Families, an Office of Child Care to serve as the principal advisor to the Assistant Secretary for Children and Families on issues regarding state-funded child care programs.
Document 4: Protecting Youth At-Risk for Sex Trafficking Act - Amends part E (Foster Care and Adoption Assistance) of title IV of the Social Security Act (SSA) to require the state agency under its foster care and adoption assistance program plan to demonstrate... policies and procedures for identifying and screening any child who the state has reasonable cause to believe is a victim of sex trafficking.
Document 5: Children in Families First Act of 2013 - Establishes within the Department of State the Bureau of Vulnerable Children and Family Security which shall: support the implementation in foreign countries of child welfare laws and policies.
Document 6: Elimination of the Single Parent Tax Act of 2009 - Amends part D (Child Support and Establishment of Paternity) of the Social Security Act to repeal the requirement that states impose a $25 annual fee for successful child support collection.
Document 7: Small Business Child Care Act - Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a program to award grants, on a competitive basis, to assist states in providing funds to encourage the establishment and operation of employer-operated child care programs.

topic.name: Federal Property and Infrastructure Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, redevelopment, and accessibility of federal properties and infrastructure, including national parks and energy facilities.

Document 1: Requires quarter dollars to have designs emblematic of national sites.
Instructs the Secretary of the Treasury to select national parks for coin designs.
Document 2: Authorizes installation of battery recharging stations in federal parking areas for employee vehicles.
Document 4: Directs redevelopment of the Department of Energy Forrestal Complex.
Requires agreements to protect U.S. interests and facilitate local redevelopment.
Document 5: Prohibits leasing buildings for federal agencies unless they meet public transportation access requirements.
Document 6: Provides for the disposition of federal property for parkland and recreational use.
Requires agreements related to the closure of a juvenile detention facility.

topic.name: Animal Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at protecting animal welfare and regulating practices related to animals in various contexts.

Document 1: prohibit entering a horse in a race that is subject to an interstate off-track wager if the person knows the horse is under the influence of a performance-enhancing drug
requires that an accredited third party conformity assessment body test the first-place horse and one additional randomly selected horse for any such drug
Document 4: amends the Horse Protection Act to replace the Designated Qualified Persons program responsible for inspecting horses for soring
prohibits a person in any horse show, horse exhibition, or horse sale or auction from causing or directing a horse to become sore
Document 5: direct the Department of the Interior and the Department of Agriculture to allow a state or an Indian tribe to assume all management and protection functions under the Act with regard to wild free-roaming horses and burros

topic.name: U.S. Trade Preference Programs
topic.description: This cluster discusses various U.S. legislative measures aimed at providing trade preferences and duty-free treatment for specific countries and products, particularly in relation to developing nations and specific industries.

Document 1: Amends the African Growth and Opportunity Act and the Trade Act of 1974 to require the President to provide duty-free treatment of all articles from qualified beneficiary countries
Effectively, provides an additional trade preference program for sub-Saharan African countries and least-developed countries.
Document 2: Amends the Andean Trade Preference Act to include Paraguay and Uruguay on the list of countries eligible for duty-free treatment
Extends preferential treatment to certain wool apparel articles.
Document 6: Directs the President to proclaim an extension through December 31, 2024, of the preferential tariff treatment provided certain apparel goods imported into the United States from Nicaragua.
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to provide for the duty-free treatment of certain recreational performance outerwear.

topic.name: Federal Coordination in Science and Health
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal initiatives aimed at enhancing coordination across various sectors of science, health, and technology to improve public policy and outcomes.

Document 1: Directs the Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) to establish or designate a working group under the National Science and Technology Council that has the responsibility of identifying and coordinating international science and technology cooperation.
Requires the working group to coordinate interagency international science and technology cooperative research and training activities and partnerships supported or managed by federal agencies.
Document 2: Establishes in the Office of the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) the Office of the National Alzheimer's Project to accelerate the development of treatments that would prevent, halt, or reverse the course of Alzheimer's.
Coordinate with international bodies to integrate and inform the fight against Alzheimer's globally.
Document 3: Establishes, within the Executive Office of the President, the Office of National Cybersecurity Advisor.
Requires the Advisor to serve as the principal advisor to the President for all cybersecurity-related matters.
Document 4: The bill expands the duties of the OFR to include publishing an annual work plan; consulting with other federal departments and agencies with relevant expertise prior to preparing any public report.
Developing and implementing a cybersecurity plan.
Document 5: Requires the Commission to conduct a comprehensive study of federal, state, local, and tribal programs that serve Native children.
Recommend modifications and improvements to programs that serve Native children at the federal, state, and tribal level.
Document 6: Establishes the National Autism Spectrum Disorders Initiative in the Office of the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to improve the lives of individuals with autism spectrum disorders through research focused on prevention, treatment, services, and cures.
Requires the Secretary to act as the primary federal official with responsibility for overseeing all NIH research on autism spectrum disorders.
Document 7: Directs the Director of the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) to establish an entrepreneur-in-residence program to appoint in-house entrepreneurs.
Assist federal agencies in improving outreach to business concerns and entrepreneurs.

topic.name: Gun Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing gun safety and regulating firearm access, particularly concerning minors and dangerous weapons.

Document 1: Requires the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to promulgate a final consumer product safety rule providing for a distinctive marking or device for any toy, look-alike, or imitation firearm to distinguish the look-alike firearm from a firearm.
Prohibits any state or political subdivision from prohibiting the sale or manufacturer of any look-alike, nonfiring to or collector replica of an antique firearm developed prior to 1898.
Document 2: Amends the Brady Handgun Violence Prevention Act to raise the age of handgun eligibility to 21 and prohibit persons under age 21 from possessing semiautomatic assault weapons.
Prohibits keeping a loaded firearm or an unloaded firearm and ammunition within any premises knowing or recklessly disregarding the risk that a child is capable of gaining access to it.

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Tribes
topic.description: This cluster discusses various acts and legislation that extend federal recognition and associated benefits to specific Native American tribes.

Document 1: Extends federal recognition to the Lumbee Tribe of North Carolina
makes its members eligible for the services and benefits provided to Indians
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to make annual disbursements to the Colorado River Indian Tribes
to fund the Office of the Colorado River Indian Tribes Reservation Energy Development
Document 3: Gives the Navajo Tribe the right to deselect not more than 757 acres of the land selected
authorizes the Navajo Tribe to designate up to 150,000 acres within specified lands to be designated as Navajo Sovereignty Empowerment Zones
Document 4: Extends federal recognition to the Muscogee Nation of Florida
Makes the Nation and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government
Document 5: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana
Makes the Tribe and each member eligible for all services and benefits provided by the United States
Document 6: Directs the Department of the Interior to take into trust for the benefit of the Samish Indian Nation
Prohibits the Tribe from conducting gaming on such land
Document 7: Amends the Ysleta del Sur Pueblo and Alabama and Coushatta Indian Tribes of Texas Restoration Act
to revise tribal membership requirements

topic.name: Greenhouse Gas Emissions Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating and reducing greenhouse gas emissions in the United States.

Document 1: California's request to regulate greenhouse gas emissions from new motor vehicles
the Clean Air Act with respect to whether such emissions...endanger public health
Document 2: prohibits the implementation of regulations for greenhouse gas emissions reductions
requires certification from other countries regarding emissions reductions
Document 3: bipartisan National Climate Solutions Commission to review actions to reduce greenhouse gas emissions
recommendations for reducing greenhouse gas emissions to the President, Congress, and the states
Document 4: authorizes federal agencies to purchase greenhouse gas offsets
requires rules regarding open market transactions involving offsets and credits
Document 5: establishes requirements for agencies to evaluate and report on short-lived climate pollutants
requires the President to establish an Interagency Task Force on Short-Lived Climate Pollutant Mitigation
Document 6: requires covered persons to purchase federal emission permits for greenhouse gas substances
establishes emission reduction targets for 2015 through 2059
Document 7: promotes air quality by providing payments to producers to address agricultural air quality concerns
requires projects to be in counties with air quality degradation from agricultural emissions

topic.name: Legislative Extensions and Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative actions involving the extension and amendment of existing laws and programs across different sectors.

Document 7: Amends the Protect America Act of 2007 to extend its authority until July 1, 2009.
States that the preceding amendment shall take effect as if enacted on February 15, 2008.

topic.name: Regulatory Oversight and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at reviewing, modifying, and overseeing federal regulations to ensure they do not adversely impact small businesses and the economy.

Document 1: Requires each federal agency to establish a plan for the periodic review of rules that have a significant economic impact on small entities.
Sets forth criteria for review of a rule, including the continued need for the rule and the economic impact on small entities.
Document 2: Prohibits covered regulations from being in effect during the time-out period.
Defines a 'covered regulation' as a final regulation that increases costs on businesses in a manner that adversely affects job creation.
Document 3: Requires the Director of the Congressional Budget Office to assess the impact of federal rules and regulations.
Provides analysis of economically significant proposed rules having an annual effect on the economy of $100 million or more.
Document 4: Prohibits the Department of Health and Human Services from finalizing proposed rules that may impact child support enforcement.
Document 5: Reduces the deadline for filing a claim seeking judicial review of a permit issued by a federal agency.
Document 6: Gives the Bureau of Land Management 45 days to review state notices for submission to the Federal Register.
Document 7: Requires an executive agency to provide notice and an opportunity for public comment before relocating agency offices.

topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at reforming federal student loan programs and providing relief to borrowers.

Document 1: Protecting Educational Loans for Underserved Students Act - Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965

Requires applicants that have been subject to such actions to participate in loan counseling provided by the applicable institution of higher education before becoming eligible for Federal Direct PLUS loans.
Document 2: Protect Student Borrowers Act of 2013 - Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
requires institutions of higher education (IHEs) participating in the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan program to accept specified risk-sharing requirements.
Document 3: Earnings Contingent Education Loans Act of 2015 or the ExCEL Act of 2015
replaces several existing federal student loan programs with a single repayment plan that caps annual debt repayments based on the borrower's income.
Document 4: Student Debt Relief Act of 2007 - Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to extend the Pell Grant program through FY2012
forgives the balance due on DLs and direct consolidation loans by individuals who have been public sector employees for 10 years.
Document 5: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require the Secretary of Education to discharge a student borrower's loan
if the borrower has been determined by the Commissioner of Social Security to be under a disability.
Document 6: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 with respect to the meaning of economic hardship
considers borrowers under such programs to be suffering economic hardship if they are working full-time, have a federal educational debt burden equal to at least 20% of their adjusted gross income.
Document 7: Veterinary Medicine Loan Repayment Program Enhancement Act
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income payments under the federal veterinary medicine loan repayment program.

topic.name: U.S. Territories Legislation
topic.description: Legislation and policies affecting U.S. territories and possessions, focusing on governance, funding, and rights.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior...
ensure that the flags of the states... encircle the Washington Monument.
Document 2: Authorizes the Attorney General to make grants to states...
for the renovation, modernization, and construction of law enforcement facilities.
Document 3: Conveys to the government of the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands submerged lands...
extending three geographical miles outward from their coastlines.
Document 4: amends the Recreation and Public Purposes Act...
to establish a pilot program that authorizes commercial recreation concessions.
Document 5: Prohibits a condominium association... from adopting or enforcing any policy...
that would restrict or prevent an association member from displaying a Service Flag.
Document 6: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 to extend the trade adjustment assistance for workers program...
to the U.S. territories of Guam, the Virgin Islands of the United States, American Samoa, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.
Document 7: Amends the Older Americans Act of 1965 to make available more funding to Guam...
for Older American community service employment programs for unemployed low-income persons over age 55.

topic.name: Opioid Regulation and Treatment
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at regulating opioid treatment, enhancing patient safety, and combating prescription drug abuse.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Administrator of the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to award grants to states and nonprofit community organizations to distribute culturally sensitive educational materials about the dangers of opioid abuse.
Establish a Controlled Substances Clinical Standards Commission to develop safe dosing standards for all forms of methadone.
Document 2: Gives an individual the right to be treated by a health care practitioner with any medical treatment that the individual desires, including a treatment that is not approved, certified, or licensed by the Secretary of Health and Human Services.
Requires a practitioner to report administering such treatment and discovering it to be a danger to an individual.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to require prescription drug packaging to incorporate radio frequency tagging technology or similar trace and track technologies.
Deems a prescription drug to be misbranded if the packaging or labeling of the drug is in violation of a requirement or prohibition of this Act.
Document 4: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to double the term of imprisonment and triple the fine for the prohibited distribution of a schedule II or schedule III controlled substance by the operator of a pill mill.

Defines 'pill mill' to include a doctor's office, clinic, or health care facility that routinely prescribes or dispenses controlled substances outside the scope of the prevailing standards of medical practice.
Document 5: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to add as schedule I controlled substances specified additional hallucinogenic substances.
Includes the salts, isomers, or salts of isomers thereof.
Document 6: Amends title XVIII of the Social Security Act with respect to prescription drug plans to repeal the prohibition against interference by the Secretary of Health and Human Services with negotiations between drug manufacturers and pharmacies.
Grants the Secretary authority similar to that of other federal entities that purchase prescription drugs in bulk to negotiate contracts with manufacturers of covered part D drugs.
Document 7: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to revise the definition of 'dispense' to include the delivery of a controlled substance through the use of an intrathecal pump by a practitioner acting in the usual course of professional practice.

topic.name: Veteran and Military Benefits
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing various benefits and protections for veterans, service members, and their families.

Document 1: Permits a veteran with any compensable service-connected disability to use commissary and exchange stores.
the veteran's dependents to use commissary and exchange stores on the same basis as a member of the Armed Forces.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the definition of 'qualified veteran' for purposes of the work opportunity tax credit.
Defines 'recently discharged veteran' to mean any individual who has served on active duty.
Document 3: Extends posthumous citizenship to an otherwise qualified noncitizen who enlisted into the U.S. Armed Forces.
Died as a result of such active duty service during the period September 1, 1939-December 31, 1946.
Document 4: Authorizes the employment of a person as a non-dual status technician.
Provides for up to 12 months of continued compensation for a military technician.
Document 5: Amends federal bankruptcy law to prohibit the bankruptcy court from dismissing a case based on means testing while the debtor is on active duty.
Requires such study to include the effects that the use by such members of this Act has upon the bankruptcy system.
Document 6: Provides employment and reemployment rights for certain individuals ordered to full-time National Guard duty.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Defense to grant access to accredited members of the media at military commemoration ceremonies.
Authorizes the Secretary to establish the policy for media accreditation.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Reform
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various aspects of U.S. immigration policies, including pathways to citizenship, protections for immigrants, and adjustments to visa categories.

Document 1: Directs the Chief of the Office of Citizenship of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to provide grants to assist legal U.S. residents who declare an intent to apply for citizenship.
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to provide that a legal U.S. resident who demonstrates English fluency will satisfy the residency requirement.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make certain nonimmigrant investors who have resided in the United States for at least five years immediately eligible to adjust to permanent resident status.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act with respect to T-visa and U-visa nonimmigrant aliens.
Revises provisions with respect to victims of crime and access to health, housing, and other public benefits.
